chapter_title.en: Chapter 200 – Praying the complete Salaat in the two Harrams (Masjid Al-Harram and Masjid Al-Medina)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/220)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/220)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ إِتْمَامِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي الْحَرَمَيْنِ فَقَالَ أُحِبُّ لَكَ مَا أُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِي أَتِمَّ الصَّلَاةَ .

en.hubeali: Yunus, from Ziyad Bin Marwan who said,
‘I asked Abu Ibrahimasws (7th Ibrahimasws) about Praying the complete Salaat in the two Harrams. So heasws said: Iasws love for youasws what Iasws love for myselfasws – the complete Salaat’.
en.sarwar: 4. Yunus has narrated from Ziyad ibn Marwan who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about performing Salat (prayer) in complete form in the two al-Haram (the sacred places), (Masjid al-Haram and Masjid of al-Madinah). He (the Imam) said, ‘I like for you what I like for myself. You should perform Salat (prayer) in complete form.’”
